project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code. Brain Platform is the core intelligence system.

1.name: Build the Agent
1.description: Reactive agent that responds when asked. Slack, memory, Google, Fireflies, sweeps, tasks.

2.name: Build the Brain
2.description: Smart memory plugin: embeddings, hybrid search, reranking, dedup, clustering, journaling, reflection. Agent-agnostic.

3.name: Build the Nervous System
3.description: Brain Platform migration: multi-brain architecture, recursive clustering, concept-aware extraction. Sidecar + connectors moving to brain-platform.

4.name: Build the Judgment
4.description: Autonomous workflows — CRM, project tracking, proactive surfacing. Good autonomous decisions.

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Sweep pipeline working. Connectors moving to brain-platform.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

G.name: GitHub + Self-Development
G.notes: Designed (three-gate safety model). Deferred until Phase 3/4 stable.

H.name: Smart Plugin Brain
H.notes: Superseded by Brain Platform (Track Q). Intelligence migrated to brain-platform repo.

M.name: Background Sweeps
M.notes: Connectors ported to brain-platform. Sidecar migration pending.

O.name: CC-Native Engine
O.notes: Claude Code as core engine via Channels.

P.name: Smart Plugin Migration
P.notes: Superseded by Brain Platform migration (Track Q).

Q.name: Brain Platform
Q.notes: Multi-brain architecture with recursive concept-aware clustering. Engine deployed, cutover done. Comms brain + sidecar migration pending.

infrastructure.local: macOS, .mcp.json runs brain-platform as local stdio process with Railway DB URL
infrastructure.database: Railway managed Postgres (automatic backups, pgvector, tsvector)
infrastructure.deployMethod: GitHub auto-deploy — clawvato from cc-native-engine branch. brain-platform via railway up (manual, auto-deploy TBD)
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — CC-native engine costs $0
infrastructure.brainPlatform: github.com/andrewcovato/brain-platform — core intelligence on Railway. Public: brain-platform-production.up.railway.app/mcp + /ingest + /health
infrastructure.brainPlatformAuth: Bearer token via MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env var

brains.primary: Business intelligence — 149 memories, 12 clusters (10 root + 2 sub). Clients, deals, strategy, relationships.
brains.dev: Technical context — 96 memories, 4 clusters. Architecture decisions, patterns, bugs, project state.
brains.comms: NOT YET CREATED — will handle email, Slack, Fireflies, calendar. Pending Step 1 of migration.

metrics.memoryClusters: 16 total (12 primary + 4 dev), recursive max depth 1

Comms brain + wire sidecar into brain-platform (NEXT)

S10.name: Memory Excellence
workstreams: WS1: Memory scoping — soft signals (domain 1.3x, surface 1.1x boost)
WS2: Write-time dedup — ADD/UPDATE/NOOP/DELETE via Haiku
WS4: Cross-encoder reranking — local ms-marco-MiniLM ($0, 48x faster)
WS5: Memory instructions — comprehensive prompt + XML-tagged context
Bonus: Entity-hop traversal, domain taxonomy, scoping refactor

workstreams: Phase 1-5: Full intelligence migration to plugin
Conversation journaling, HDBSCAN clustering, agent wiring

workstreams: Journal hook + insights scratch pad + brief auto-update
Sonnet extraction upgrade + plugin sync command
Sidecar rebuild: tiered sweeps, event feed, -685 lines

S27.name: Comms Brain + Real-Time Ingest
goals: Newmail integration with comms brain clusters

lastSession.type: Brain Platform — build, deploy, cutover, recursive clustering
keyDecisions: brain-platform is the core system, not a plugin. CC is one adapter.
Logical brain separation (brain_id column, same DB)
Default brain_id = 'primary' (60% of memories are business intelligence)
Three flow types: feeds UP, context DOWN, drill-downs
Concepts are per-brain YAML config → extraction prompts auto-generated
Recursive HDBSCAN with adaptive depth (HDBSCAN decides when to stop)
Cluster identity preservation via centroid similarity matching
Stay in clawvato directory for sessions

immediateBacklog.title: Create comms brain + wire sidecar into brain-platform (Migration Step 1)
Remove sweep logic from clawvato sidecar (Migration Step 2)

backlog.title: Feed engine: comms → primary (filtered high-value facts)
Entity resolution system (Phil Clark = Phil = phil@acorns.com)
Track G Phase 1: read-only GitHub access

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE CODE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md (or brain.yaml)
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Always build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Check memory before researching — don't explore when the answer is in context
Brain stores intelligence + source pointers, NOT content. Source APIs store content.
Concepts are per-brain config, not global. Each brain has different understanding of the world.
Exclusive source ownership: each raw source has exactly one brain owner
Default brain_id = 'primary' — business intelligence is the majority, dev is the minority
Recursive HDBSCAN: let HDBSCAN decide when to stop recursing, don't force fixed depth
Composite clustering text: [concept_type] entities: content — separates structure from semantics
Cluster identity preservation: match new→old by centroid similarity, don't wipe IDs every re-cluster
postgres.js tagged templates silently drop ::vector cast on INSERT — use INSERT then UPDATE
Entity parsing: DB stores entities as JSON string, always parse defensively
Always soft-delete memories (valid_until = NOW()), never hard DELETE
MCP SDK Server.connect() is 1:1 — new Server+Transport per HTTP request
Journal hook uses /ingest REST endpoint (not MCP) — fire-and-forget
Sonnet > Haiku for extraction quality
Two schedulers: brain (consolidation, reflection, clustering, decay) and agent (user tasks)
Sweeps: HWM + dedup = two-layer idempotency
